title: Spermacoce schumanniana
taxonomy: Spermacoce schumanniana belongs to the family Rubiaceae. It is a species of flowering plant in the genus Spermacoce. The genus Spermacoce is a part of the order Gentianales. This classification indicates the evolutionary relationships between Spermacoce schumanniana and other related species. Spermacoce schumanniana is often placed within the tribe Spermacoceae.
uses: Spermacoce schumanniana, also known as the "false buttonweed," has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. Its medicinal uses include the treatment of fever, cough, and respiratory issues. In some cultures, it is used to make remedies for colds and flu. The plant's leaves and stems are typically used in infusions or decoctions to extract its active compounds. Further research is needed to fully understand the efficacy and potential side effects of using Spermacoce schumanniana as a medicinal plant.

intro: Spermacoce schumanniana, also known as Schumann's Spermacoce, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. This plant is traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.
